title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
content_en: This manager used to accumulate his workload and take upon his shoulders burdens he could not bear. One day, he called his secretary, so he entered and stood in front of him. The secretary said, вАЬYes. How may I help, sir?вАЭ He said, вАЬI called your phone but you never answered!вАЭ The secretary replied, вАЬThat was because I was in the other room. I am sorry, sir!вАЭ He replied, вАЬYou are always saying sorry! Take these papers and give them to the head of the repair department, and return quickly!вАЭ The secretary, being annoyed, went to the ofпђБce of the repair departmentвАЩs head and threw the papers on his desk and said, вАЬDo not take long with these papers!вАЭ The man became irritated at the secretaryвАЩs behaviour and said, вАЬOK, but please, place them on the table appropriately!вАЭ The secretary replied, вАЬAppropriately or inappropriately, it does not matter! What is important is that you пђБnish with the papers quickly!вАЭ They argued and insulted each other until voices were raised, after which the secretary went back to his ofпђБce.After a couple of hours, one of the minor workers in the repair department went to his Head and said, вАЬI will now go to collect my children from school and return shortly.вАЭ The Head shouted saying, вАШYou go out everyday!вАЭ He replied, вАШThis is what I have been doing for ten years! This is the пђБrst time you are objecting.вАЭ The Head said, вАЬYou only listen when you are being told off! Go back to your ofпђБce!вАЭ The poor man went to his ofпђБce, perplexed at this attitude, and began to call people to arrange someone to pick up his children from the school and take them home, but found nobody. The children had to stand for a long time in the sun until one of the teachers decided to take them home. When this employee returned home angry, his youngest son came to him with a toy and said, вАЬDad! My teacher gave this to me, because IвА¶вАЭ вАЬGo to your mum!вАЭ says the father, interrupting him and pushing him away. The child goes to his mother crying, and on his way, his beautiful cat comes to him and begins to rub itself against his legs as usual, but he kicks the cat with his foot and the cat ends up hitting the wall. Who kicked the cat? Question: Who kicked the cat? Perhaps you will smile and say, вАЬThe manager.вАЭ ThatвАЩs right, it is the manager, because he pressurised himself so much that he eventually exploded. Why do we not learn the skill of distributing our workload? Why canвАЩt we say with courage about the tasks that we cannot perform, вАЬThis is out of our handsвА¶ We arenвАЩt able to do thisвА¶вАЭ especially when placing yourself under pressure may result in you behaving in a way that adversely affects people who were not part of the problem to begin with. Be careful to not allow yourself to be pressurised by others, such that they embarrass you until you give in to their demands, or make empty promises. Come with me to Madinah, if you please, and look at the Messenger of Allah пБ•, as he sat in his blessed gathering, after Islam had spread far and wide and the people worshipped Allah alone. The leaders of various tribes came to him in submission as believers, whilst some came to him disguising hate and malice in their hearts. One day, one of the Arab leaders came to him. He was very well respected amongst his people. His name was вАШAmir bin al-Tufayl. His people, when they noticed how Islam had spread, would suggest to him, вАЬO вАШAmir, most people have become Muslim. You may as well embrace Islam.вАЭ вАШAmir was very arrogant and haughty. He would say to them in response, вАЬBy Allah, I have already sworn that I will not die until the Arabs make me their king and follow me, for I am not Who kicked the cat? going to follow this young man from Quraysh!вАЭ Later when he saw that Islam had dominated and the people had agreed to follow the Messenger of Allah пБ•, he mounted his camel and went along with his companions to the Messenger of Allah пБ•. When he entered the mosque, he saw the Messenger of Allah пБ• sitting with his Companions. He stood in front of the Prophet пБ• and said, вАЬO Muhammad! I want to speak to you alone.вАЭ The Prophet пБ• was cautious of such people, so he said, вАЬNo, by Allah! Not until you believe in Allah alone.вАЭ He said again, вАЬO Muhammad, I want to speak to you alone.вАЭ But the Prophet пБ• refused. But he kept insisting, вАЬO Muhammad, come with me so I may speak to you!вАЭ So the Prophet пБ• eventually got up to speak to him. As he got up, вАШAmir pulled one of his companions over to him. His name was Arbad, whom вАШAmir conspired with to kill the Messenger of Allah пБ•.
